title: Why does my microcontroller reset when the motor starts?

summary.headline: Most likely issue: Local bulk capacitance is missing or too small.
summary.detail: Next measurement: Measure the voltage between MCU ground and motor return.

exportSummary.scopeNote: Scoped to common low-voltage educational and prototype circuits.
exportSummary.nextProbe: Measure the voltage between MCU ground and motor return
exportSummary.firstFix: Add bulk capacitance near the motor driver
exportSummary.oneLine: MCU + Motor Brownout with symptom "MCU resets" most likely points to local bulk capacitance being too small at 84% confidence.
exportSummary.title: Bench-ready summary
exportSummary.likelyIssue: Local bulk capacitance is missing or too small

rankedFaults.label: Local bulk capacitance is missing or too small
rankedFaults.description: Transient current steals charge from the logic rail.
fixes: Add bulk capacitance near the motor driver

healthyObservations: Regulator input stayed close to battery.

measurements.detail: The source dips hard under inrush.
measurements.title: Battery under load
